items.question: Which attribute is used to force all characters in a TextView to be displayed in uppercase?
items.explanation: The android:textAllCaps attribute, when set to true, forces the text to be displayed in uppercase letters regardless of how it is written in the source string.

items.question: What is the correct syntax to reference a string named 'welcome_msg' defined in the strings.xml file?
items.explanation: In Android XML layouts, resources from the values folder are accessed using the '@' symbol followed by the resource type and name.

items.question: A developer wants an ImageView to scale an image uniformly while preserving its aspect ratio so that it completely fills the view, even if it means cropping parts of the image. Which scaleType should be used?
items.explanation: CENTER_CROP scales the image uniformly while preserving aspect ratio so it completely fills the view, possibly cropping excess parts.

items.question: Predict the visual behavior of the following TextView if the text is longer than the screen width:
items.code_snippet:
<TextView
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="wrap_content"
    android:maxLines="1"
    android:ellipsize="end"
    android:text="This is a very long text that will definitely exceed the screen width." />
items.answer: The text will be restricted to one line and end with '...'
choices: The text will wrap to a second line
The text will be cut off abruptly at the edge
The text will be restricted to one line and end with '...'
The text will scroll horizontally like a marquee
items.explanation: Combining android:maxLines="1" and android:ellipsize="end" ensures the text stays on one line and displays three dots at the end if truncated.

items.question: Identify the error in the following XML snippet for a password input field:
items.code_snippet:
<EditText
    android:id="@+id/pass_field"
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="wrap_content"
    android:text="Enter Password"
    android:inputType="password" />
items.answer: Using 'android:text' instead of 'android:hint' for placeholder text
items.explanation: In an EditText, android:hint should be used for placeholder text that disappears when the user types. Using android:text would make 'Enter Password' the actual editable content.

items.question: Correct the following XML code to properly use a dimension resource for the text size.
items.explanation: Text size should include units (sp) or reference a resource from dimens.xml (e.g., @dimen/name). Hardcoding '24' without units is invalid.

items.question: Which attribute allows an ImageView to resize itself to maintain its image's aspect ratio when its height or width is set to wrap_content?
items.explanation: android:adjustViewBounds="true" ensures the aspect ratio is maintained when resizing the ImageView, provided one dimension is set to wrap_content.

items.question: What happens if a developer sets 'android:visibility' to 'invisible' for a Button?
items.answer: The button is not visible but still takes up space in the layout
choices: The button is completely removed from the layout
The button is not visible but still takes up space in the layout
The button is visible but cannot be clicked
items.explanation: View.INVISIBLE means the view is not visible but still occupies the same space it would if it were visible.

items.question: Find and fix the syntax error in this Button declaration:
items.answer: Missing 'android:' prefix for layout_width and layout_height
items.explanation: Standard Android attributes like width and height must be prefixed with the 'android:' namespace.

items.question: Write the XML code for a CheckBox that is checked by default and displays the text 'Accept Terms'.
items.explanation: The android:text attribute sets the label, and android:checked="true" sets the initial state.

items.question: In the context of accessibility, what is the primary purpose of the 'android:contentDescription' attribute for an ImageView?
items.answer: To provide a text description for screen readers
choices: To define the title of the image
To provide a text description for screen readers
To show a caption below the image
To set the metadata of the image file
items.explanation: android:contentDescription is essential for accessibility as it provides a verbal description of the image for users with visual impairments using screen readers.

items.question: Fix the error in this ImageView intended to display a background pattern that repeats.
items.answer: The 'tileMode' attribute is for <bitmap> resources, not ImageView. Use a bitmap drawable as src.
items.explanation: Repeating patterns are defined within a <bitmap> XML resource using android:tileMode="repeat". An ImageView itself does not have a tileMode attribute; it simply displays the drawable provided to it [480, 222-224 (referenced context from Ch2)].

items.question: Which file in the values folder is specifically designed to store hexadecimal color values to encourage UI consistency?
items.explanation: The colors.xml file is used to centralize and reuse color definitions across the app.

items.question: Identify why using 'sp' is preferred over 'dp' for the 'android:textSize' property of a TextView.
items.answer: sp scales based on user font size preferences for accessibility
choices: sp is more precise on high-density screens
sp is required for custom fonts
sp scales based on user font size preferences for accessibility
dp only works for layout dimensions, not text
items.explanation: While both are density-independent, 'sp' (scalable pixels) also factors in the system-wide font size scale chosen by the user for accessibility.

items.question: Write an XML snippet for an EditText that only accepts email addresses and displays 'Email' as a hint.
items.explanation: The android:inputType="textEmailAddress" attribute configures the keyboard for email entry, and android:hint provides the placeholder.

items.question: What is the result of using 'android:scaleType="centerInside"' on an image that is smaller than the ImageView boundaries?
items.answer: The image stays at its original size and is centered
choices: The image is stretched to fill the boundaries
The image stays at its original size and is centered
The image is cropped to fit the center
The image aspect ratio is ignored to fill the width
items.explanation: CENTER_INSIDE scales the image down to fit if it's larger than the view, but if it's smaller, it remains at its original size and is centered without scaling up.

items.question: Debug this ScrollView usage:
items.code_snippet:
<ScrollView
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="match_parent">
    <Button
        android:layout_width="match_parent"
        android:layout_height="wrap_content"
        android:text="Button 1" />
    <Button
        android:layout_width="match_parent"
        android:layout_height="wrap_content"
        android:text="Button 2" />
</ScrollView>
items.answer: A ScrollView can only have one direct child view.
items.explanation: A ScrollView is a container that can hold only one direct child. Multiple views must be wrapped inside a single layout (like LinearLayout) which is then placed inside the ScrollView.

items.question: Code a vertical 'Space' view with a height of 50dp to be used between two buttons.
items.explanation: The Space view is an invisible component used to add gaps. Setting the height defines the vertical distance.
